input.prompt:
I'm worried these tests might have problems. Audit
`Payments.Tests/PaymentProcessorTests.cs` for .NET test anti-patterns
using the full anti-pattern catalog (missing assertions, swallowed
exceptions, always-true/self-comparing assertions, flakiness, magic
values, duplicate tests, broad exception types, etc.) and tell me
what (if anything) genuinely needs fixing — be careful not to invent
issues that aren't really there.

Give me a severity-ranked list (Critical / Warning / Info / "no
issues found"). Each finding must be tied to a named anti-pattern
from the catalog and include a concrete code-level fix. Do not edit
the files — this is a read-only review.

data.reasoningText: Let me read the test file to audit it for anti-patterns.

data.content:
## Audit Result: **No issues found** ✅

This is a well-written test class. Here's why none of the common anti-patterns apply:

| Anti-pattern | Verdict |
|---|---|
| **Missing assertions** | Every test has meaningful assertions on the result |
| **Swallowed exceptions** | No bare `try/catch`; exceptions are verified via `ThrowsException<T>` |
| **Always-true / self-comparing** | Assertions compare actual results against distinct expected values |
| **Broad exception types** | Uses specific `ArgumentOutOfRangeException`, not `Exception` |
| **Magic values** | Amounts/order-IDs are simple inline literals appropriate for unit tests — no unexplained constants |
| **Duplicate tests** | Zero/negative amount tests look similar but cover distinct boundary conditions |
| **Flakiness** | No dates, randomness, threading, or external I/O (uses `FakeGateway`) |
| **Test logic in production code** | Clean Arrange/Act/Assert with a test double |

The tests are concise, deterministic, use exact exception types, assert on specific outcomes, and parameterize currency variants with `[DataRow]`. Nothing here warrants a fix.
